does: This paper surveys the field of jailbreaking attacks against large language models and vision-language models, providing a systematic taxonomy of attack methods organized into seven distinct categories. The authors conduct a comprehensive empirical and conceptual review of how adversarial prompts and techniques can circumvent safety mechanisms and ethical boundaries in these systems, while simultaneously examining corresponding defense strategies designed to mitigate such attacks. The work identifies specific vulnerabilities across different model architectures and safety training approaches, establishing a structured landscape of current jailbreaking techniques and their countermeasures through detailed categorization and analysis.
connects: This work builds on earlier research into adversarial attacks on neural networks and safety alignment in machine learning systems, extending those foundations to the specific challenges posed by large-scale language and vision models. It responds to growing concerns about prompt injection attacks, role-playing exploits, and logical manipulation techniques that emerged as LLMs gained widespread adoption. The survey synthesizes insights from parallel work on AI safety, red-teaming methodologies, and alignment verification, while connecting to foundational research on model robustness and adversarial examples that has been central to deep learning security since their discovery in computer vision.
enables: This survey establishes a structured framework that enables future work in developing more sophisticated defense mechanisms tailored to specific jailbreak categories. By providing a unified taxonomy of attacks and defenses, it points toward developing better safety evaluation benchmarks and red-teaming protocols for assessing model robustness before deployment. The work facilitates research into more fundamental alignment approaches that could make models inherently resistant to jailbreaking rather than relying solely on surface-level behavioral constraints. Additionally, it enables the development of automated detection systems for jailbreak attempts and supports the creation of more comprehensive safety training procedures that address the full spectrum of identified attack vectors across both language and vision modalities.
